The sunroof shattered while driving down the freeway. No vehicles near or overpasses near. The shade was closed so the glass fell on it not on my head or passengers that could have been present. Sounded like a loud pop. Then I heard wind noise. The glass is blown upward, not down

narrative.headline: 2020 Toyota Tundra: acceleration and engine complaints lead the list
narrative.summary: Owners filed 44 complaints with NHTSA, and the volume is low and spread fairly evenly. The fuel/propulsion system tops the list at 14%, with the engine close behind at 11% — both centered on weak or hesitant acceleration. Steering and service brakes follow at 9% each. No fires, injuries, or deaths are reported; the only crash (1) is tied to the brakes, so that area is worth a closer look on a test drive.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 6 reports (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners describe weak or hesitant acceleration when pressing the gas pedal.

issues.sample: I BOUGHT A NEW 2020 TOYOTA TUNDRA 4X4 CREWMAX AND IT IS NOT PERFORM TO WHAT A V-8 POWERTRAIN OF 5.7 LT, AND 381 HORSE POWER SHOULD BE.  THE ACCELERATOR ON THIS 2020 TUNDRA IS NOT FUNCTIONING PROPERLY.  IT WILL NOT ACCELERATE WHEN I STEP ON THE GAS PEDAL, WHICH ALMOST STALL BEFORE
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 5 reports (11%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. The complaints echo the acceleration and power-delivery problems noted above.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 4 reports (9%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners mention front-end vibration or bounce — feel for it on a test drive.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 4 reports (9%) and the only crash in the data (1), with no fires or injuries. Test the brakes carefully given that crash link.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: The fewest reports here: 3 (7%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. A small but recurring complaint area.
